volumeTitle: Casina. The Casket Comedy. Curculio. Epidicus. The Two Menaechmuses
label: Casina (Section 3.1.526b - Section 3.4.615)

sections.label: Section 3.1.526b
sections.original: Bene ambula .
sections.translation: Luck go with you .

sections.label: Section 3.1.527
sections.original: Fac habeant linguam tuae aedes .
sections.translation: Take care that your house gets a tongue .

sections.label: Section 3.1.527b
sections.original: Quid ita ?
sections.translation: Why so ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.527c
sections.original: Cum veniam , vocent .
sections.translation: That when I come , it may invite me .

sections.label: Section 3.1.528
sections.original: Attatae , caedundus tú homo es : nimias delicias facis .
sections.translation: Pooh , pooh ! you area person that stands in good need of a basting ; you ’ re making too free with your fun .

sections.label: Section 3.1.529
sections.original: Quid me amare refert , nisi sim doctus ac dicaculus ?
sections.translation: Of what use is it for me to be in love , unless I ’ m quite ready and talkative ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.530
sections.original: sed tu cave in quaesitione mihi sis .—
sections.translation: But take you care that you haven ’ t to be sought for by me .

sections.label: Section 3.1.530b
sections.original: Vsque adero domi .—
sections.translation: I ’ ll be at home all the while .

sections.label: Section 3.2.531
sections.original: Hoc erat ecastor quod me vir tanto opere orabat meus ,
sections.translation: This was the reason , then , i ’ faith , why my husband entreated me , with such great earnestness , to make haste and invite my female neighbour to our house — that the house might be clear for him to be taking Casina there . Now , therefore , I shall by no means invite her ,

sections.label: Section 3.2.535
sections.original: liberi loci potestas sit , vetulis vervecibus .
sections.translation: so that liberty of free range shan ’ t be any way given to worn-out bell-wethers . ( ALCESIMUS is coming out of his house .) But look , the pillar of the Senate ’ s coming forward , the safeguard of the public , my neighbour , the person who is finding free range for my husband . I ’ faith , the measure of wit [*] that has been sold to him , was purchased at no cheap rate .

sections.label: Section 3.2.539
sections.original: Miror huc iam non arcessi in proxumum uxorem meam ,
sections.translation: I ’ m wondering that my wife , who ’ s already waiting at home , dressed out , to be sent for , hasn ’ t been invited by this to my neighbour ’ s here .

sections.label: Section 3.2.541
sections.original: sed eccam , opino arcessit . salve , Cleostrata .
sections.translation: But see , here she is ; she ’ s come to fetch her , I guess . ( Going up to CLEOSTRATA .) Good day , Cleostrata !

sections.label: Section 3.2.541b
sections.original: Et tu , Alcesime .
sections.translation: And you the same , Alcesimus . Where ’ s your wife ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.542b
sections.original: Intus illa te , si se arcessas , manet ;
sections.translation: She ’ s waiting in-doors for you to send for her ; for your husband requested me to send her to help you . Do you wish me to call her ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.544b
sections.original: Sine eam ⋯ nolo si occupata est .
sections.translation: Let it alone ; I don ’ t care ; ⋯ if she ’ s busy .

sections.label: Section 3.2.544c
sections.original: Otium est .
sections.translation: She ’ s at leisure .

sections.label: Section 3.2.545
sections.original: Nil moror , molesta ei esse nolo ; post convenero .
sections.translation: I don ’ t care about it ; I don ’ t want to be troublesome to her ; I ’ ll see her at a future time .

sections.label: Section 3.2.546
sections.original: Non ornatis istic apud vos nuptias ?
sections.translation: Are you not getting ready for a wedding there at your house ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.546b
sections.original: Orno et paro .
sections.translation: I am getting ready and making preparations .

sections.label: Section 3.2.547
sections.original: Non ergo opus est adiutrice ?
sections.translation: Don ’ t you require an assistant then ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.547b
sections.original: Satis domist . ubi nuptiae
sections.translation: We have enough at home . When the marriage has taken place , then I ’ ll call upon her ; for the present , farewell , and bid her the same from me . ( Goes into her house .)

sections.label: Section 3.2.549
sections.original: Quid ego nunc faciam ? flagitium maxumum feci miser ,
sections.translation: What am I to do now ? To my sorrow . I ’ ve done a most disgraceful action

sections.label: Section 3.2.550
sections.original: propter operam illius hirqui ímprobi , edéntuli ,
sections.translation: for the sake of that vile and toothless goat , who has engaged me in this . I ’ ve promised the aid of my wife out of doors , as though to go lick dishes [*] like a dog . A worthless fellow , to tell me that his wife was going to send for her , whereas she herself declares that she does not want her . And upon my faith , it ’ s a wonder if this female neighbour of mine hasn ’ t already her suspicions of this .

sections.label: Section 3.2.555
sections.original: verum autem altrovorsum quóm eam mecum rationem puto ,
sections.translation: But yet , on the other hand , when I reflect with myself on this notion , if there were anything of that , there would have been enquiries of me . I ’ ll go in-doors , that I may lay up the ship [*] again in the dockyard .

sections.label: Section 3.2.558
sections.original: Iám hic est lepide ludificatus . miseri ut festinant senes .
sections.translation: Now he has been finely made a fool of . In what a bustle are these unfortunate old fellows . Now I do wish that that good-for-nothing decrepit husband of mine would come ,

sections.label: Section 3.2.560
sections.original: ut eum ludificem vicissim , postquam hunc delusi alterum .
sections.translation: that I might make a fool of him in his turn , after I have thus fooled the other one . For I long to make a bit of a quarrel between these two . But look , he ’ s coming . Why , when you see him so serious , you ’ d think him a decent person . ( She stands on one side , unseen .)

sections.label: Section 3.3.563
sections.original: Stultitia magna est , mea quidem sententia ,
sections.translation: It ’ s a great folly , to my notion at least , for any man that ’ s in love to go to the Forum

sections.label: Section 3.3.565
sections.original: in eum diem quoi quod amet in mundo siet ;
sections.translation: on that day on which the object which he loves is close at hand [*] ; as I in my folly have been doing ; I ’ ve spent the day , standing pleading [*] for a certain relative of mine , who , faith , I ’ m very delighted has lost his cause ; so that he hasn ’ t for nothing chosen me as his advocate to-day .

sections.label: Section 3.3.570
sections.original: nam meo quidem animo qui advocatos advocet
sections.translation: He ought first to ask and make enquiry , whether his mind is at home or not at home , whom he ’ s choosing for his advocate ; if he says it isn ’ t , without his mind he should send him off home . ( Catches sight of his wife .) But look , there ’ s my wife before the house ! Alas ! wretch that I am !

sections.label: Section 3.3.575
sections.original: metuo ne non sit surda atque haec audiverit .
sections.translation: I ’ m afraid that she isn ’ t deaf , and has heard this .

sections.label: Section 3.3.576
sections.original: Audivi ecastor cum malo magno tuo .
sections.translation: By my troth , I have heard it to your great cost .

sections.label: Section 3.3.577
sections.original: Accedam propius . quid agis , mea festivitas ?
sections.translation: I ’ ll go nearer to her . ( Goes up to CLEOSTRATA .) What are you about , my delight ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.578
sections.original: Te ecastor praestolabar .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I was waiting for you .

sections.label: Section 3.3.578b
sections.original: Iamne ornata res ?
sections.translation: Are the things ready now ? Have you by this brought over here to our house this female neighbour of yours ,

sections.label: Section 3.3.580
sections.original: quae te adiutaret ?
sections.translation: who was to assist you ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.580b
sections.original: Arcessivi , ut iusseras .
sections.translation: I sent for her as you requested me ; but this companion of yours , your very good friend , was in a pet with his wife about something , I don ’ t know what ; he said , when I went to fetch her , that he wouldn ’ t send her .

sections.label: Section 3.3.584
sections.original: Vitium tibi istuc maxumum est , blanda es parum .
sections.translation: That ’ s your greatest fault ; you are not courteous enough .

sections.label: Section 3.3.585
sections.original: Non matronarum officiumst , sed meretricium ,
sections.translation: It ’ s not the part of matrons , but of harlots , to be showing courtesies , my husband , to the husbands of others . Go yourself and fetch her ; I wish to attend in-doors , my husband , to what is requisite to be done .

sections.label: Section 3.3.588b
sections.original: Propera ergo .
sections.translation: Make haste then .

sections.label: Section 3.3.588c
sections.original: Licet .
sections.translation: Very well . ( Aside .) Now , faith , I shall inspire some apprehensions in his heart .

sections.label: Section 3.3.590
sections.original: miserrumum hodie ego hunc habebo amasium .—
sections.translation: I ’ ll this day render this lovesick man completely miserable .

sections.label: Section 3.4.591
sections.original: Viso huc , amator si a foro rediit domum ,
sections.translation: I ’ ll go see here if the lover has come back home from the Forum , who , an old ghost , has been making fools of myself and my wife . But see , there he is before his house . ( Addressing STALINO .) I ’ faith , ’ twas just in good time I was coming to your house .

sections.label: Section 3.4.594
sections.original: Et hercle ego ad te . quid ais , vir minimi preti ?
sections.translation: And , i ’ faith , I to yours . How say you , you good-for-nothing fellow ? What did I enjoin you ? What did I beg of you ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.595b
sections.original: Quid est ?
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.596
sections.original: Vt bene vocivas aedis fecisti mihi ,
sections.translation: How nicely you ’ ve had your house empty for me ! How well you have sent your wife over to our house here ! Isn ’ t it through yourself that I and the opportunity are lost , both of us ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.599
sections.original: Quin tu suspendis te ? nempe túte dixeras ,
sections.translation: Why don ’ t you go hang yourself ? Why , ’ twas you yourself said that

sections.label: Section 3.4.600
sections.original: tuam arcessituram esse uxorem uxorem meam .
sections.translation: your wife would come and fetch mine from our house ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.601
sections.original: Ergo arcessivisse ait sese , et dixisse te
sections.translation: Then she declares that she has been to fetch her , and that you said you wouldn ’ t let her go .

sections.label: Section 3.4.602b
sections.original: Quin eapse ultro mihi
sections.translation: But she herself , of her own accord , said to me that she didn ’ t care for her assistance .

sections.label: Section 3.4.604
sections.original: Quin eapse me adlegavit , qui istam arcesserem .
sections.translation: But ’ tis she [*] herself who has deputed me to come and fetch her .

sections.label: Section 3.4.605
sections.original: Quin nihili facio .
sections.translation: But I don ’ t care for that .

sections.label: Section 3.4.605b
sections.original: Quin me perdis .
sections.translation: But you are proving my ruin .

sections.label: Section 3.4.605c
sections.original: Quin benest ,
sections.translation: But that ’ s as it should be . But I shall still go on delaying ; but I very much long for —

sections.label: Section 3.4.607
sections.original: Quin —
sections.translation: But —

sections.label: Section 3.4.607b
sections.original: Aliquid aegre facere .
sections.translation: nothing but to do you some mischief ;

sections.label: Section 3.4.607c
sections.original: Quin faciam lubens .
sections.translation: but I ’ ll do it with pleasure . Never this day shall you have a but the more than I .

sections.label: Section 3.4.609
sections.original: Quin hercle di te perdant postremo quidem .
sections.translation: But , in fine , really , upon my faith , may the Gods confound you .

sections.label: Section 3.4.610
sections.original: Quid nunc ? missurusne es ad me uxorem tuam ?
sections.translation: What now ? Are you going to send your wife to my house ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.611
sections.original: Ducas , easque in maxumam malam crucem
sections.translation: You may take her , and be off to utter and extreme perdition , both with her and with that one of yours , and with that mistress of yours as well . A way with you , and attend to something else ; I ’ ll at once bid my wife to pass thither through the garden to your wife .

sections.label: Section 3.4.615
sections.original: Nunc tu mi amicus es in germanum modum .
sections.translation: Now you are proving yourself a friend to me in genuine style ! ( ALCESIMUS goes into his house .) Under what auspices am I to say that this passion was inflicted upon me , or what have I ever done amiss towards Venus , that when I ’ m thus in love crosses so many should befall me in my path ? ( A noise is heard .) Hey day !
